dialogue_line_replacements.text: Oye, ¿tú quieres venir a una barbacoa el sábado?
dialogue_line_replacements.translation: Hey, do you want to come to a barbecue on Saturday? (PR pronoun order)

dialogue_line_replacements.text: ¡Claro que sí! Entre más, mejor. Que venga con su familia.
dialogue_line_replacements.translation: Of course! The more the merrier. He can come with his family.

cultural_notes_overrides.formality: In Puerto Rico, social invitations are often last-minute and spontaneous. 'Cae por la casa' (drop by the house) is a common casual invitation that means you're welcome anytime.
cultural_notes_overrides.gestures: A kiss on the cheek when arriving at a social event is standard in Puerto Rico, even on first meeting in informal settings.
cultural_notes_overrides.regional_variations: 'Tirar para' means to head/go to a place: '¿Tiramos para la playa?' (Shall we head to the beach?). Very common in PR for making plans.
